questions.question: A right triangle has an angle of 35° and the side opposite to it is 12 cm. What is the length of the hypotenuse? Round to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: Use SOH-CAH-TOA: sin(35°) = opposite/hypotenuse = 12/h. Solve h = 12/sin(35°) ≈ 12/0.574 ≈ 20.9 cm.

questions.question: If cos(θ) = 3/5 and θ is in the first quadrant, find sin(θ).
questions.explanation: Use the Pythagorean identity sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1. Then sin²(θ) = 1 − (3/5)² = 1 − 9/25 = 16/25, so sin(θ) = 4/5 (positive in quadrant I).

questions.question: A ladder leans against a wall, forming a 60° angle with the ground. If the ladder is 10 feet long, how far up the wall does it reach?
questions.explanation: The height is the side opposite the 60° angle. Use sin(60°) = height/10, so height = 10 · sin(60°) = 10 · (√3/2) = 5√3 feet.

questions.question: Simplify: tan(θ) · cos(θ)
questions.explanation: Rewrite tan(θ) = sin(θ)/cos(θ). Then tan(θ) · cos(θ) = (sin(θ)/cos(θ)) · cos(θ) = sin(θ).

questions.question: On the unit circle, a point is at angle 150°. What are its coordinates?
questions.explanation: At 150°, x = cos(150°) = −cos(30°) = −√3/2 and y = sin(150°) = sin(30°) = 1/2 (reference angle is 30° in quadrant II).

questions.question: A surveyor stands 50 meters from a building and measures the angle of elevation to the roof as 40°. Estimate the height of the building to the nearest meter.
questions.explanation: Use tan(40°) = height/50. Then height = 50 · tan(40°) ≈ 50 · 0.839 ≈ 42 meters.
